titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Cornelius Eady Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12232319.
This collection contains 58 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 2 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 26, 2025 at 23:01
Finding aid revised to contextualize oppressive and harmful descriptive language. See the processing note for more information.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: 42 boxes
did.abstract: The Cornelius Eady Papers contains writings, correspondence, printed material, electronic files, and audiovisual material that document the professional activities of African American poet, author, and educator Cornelius Eady.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cornelius Eady Papers contain writings, correspondence, electronic records, audiovisual material, and printed material created and accumulated by American poet and educator Cornelius Eady, documenting his life and work as well as literary organizations including Cave Canem, and African American arts and letters broadly.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order, as received by the Beinecke Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 37-40 (computer discs and audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 41 (Student evaluations): Restricted until 2094. Consult the appropriate curator for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Cornelius Eady Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Cornelius Eady (1954-)
bioghist.p: Cornelius Eady, American poet, born 1954. In 1996 he co-founded Cave Canem, a nonprofit organization dedicated to the cultivation of African American poets.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe Rare Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Cornelius Eady Papers. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of Americaln Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cornelius Eady Papers contains writings, correspondence, printed material, electronic files, and audiovisual material that document the professional activities of African American poet, author, and educator Cornelius Eady. Writings span the length of Eady's career from his first published book of poetry, Kartunes (West Orange, N.J.: Warthog Press, 1980), to the collection Hardheaded Weather (New York: G.P. Putnam's Sons, 2008). In addition to poetry, writings include numerous drafts and associated material documenting his collaborative works of musical theater: You Don't Miss Your Water , Running Man , Fangs , and Brutal Imagination .

Other papers in the collection document Eady's involvement in American arts and letters through his contributions to educational and professional organizations, especially Cave Canem, a nonprofit organization dedicated to the cultivation of African American poets. Correspondence and Writings of Others include numerous poetry manuscripts accumulated by Eady in the course of his activities as a teacher and mentor to other poets.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Cave Canem Records (JWJ MSS 130). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection contains files for the Community of Writers. The Community of Writers previously used the oppressive and harmful term "squaw" in its name, based on its location in what was formerly known as "Squaw Valley," California, now called Olympic Valley. This language has been retained due to its use at the time the materials were created and to promote searchability and discoverability of the collections. The use of this name is not an endorsement of the language it contains.

did.unittitle: "The Writer's Voice of Billings YMCA," grant application
"Dreaming in Hi-Fi," songs by Cornelius Eady

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "The Shirt Off Your Back," biographies, pictures, and tentative orders (mailing list)
Writings sent to Sarah Micklem by M. Wilber
"The War Against The Obvious: New and Selected Poems by Cornelius Eady"
"Lucky House: Poems on a New House"
Poems, drafts, mostly from Kartunes and The Modern World
"Every room we ever slept in," poems by Jason Shinder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, notes, ephemera, records of boards, organizations, and grant programs that Eady was involved in (including the National Writers' Voice Project and the National Endowment for the Humanities), and papers relating to Stony Brook University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file pertains to the Community of Writers. For more information on the language used in this file, see the collection's processing note.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Static and high amounts of buzz and hum are present. Audio, at times, sounds hollow or unclear. Audio is unbalanced. Dropouts in the audio are present.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: High background noise is present. Static can be heard. The program is distorted at times. There are dropouts in the program. The program ends abruptly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note reads: I'm sorry it took so long to get this to you! I hope you enjoy the tape. The event was beautiful, and as always, you were welcoming and articulate. It's a pleasure to work with you.
